openmeter/ledger/routingrules.purpose: Pure validation layer for double-entry ledger transactions: defines the RoutingRule interface, a composable Validator, and DefaultValidator encoding all permitted account-type pairings and route-field constraints. No DB access — validates []ledger.EntryInput before any persistence call.

patterns.name: RoutingRule interface + Validator composition
patterns.description: Each constraint is a named struct implementing RoutingRule{Validate(TxView) error}. Validator.ValidateEntries runs them sequentially, fail-fast on first error. New rules are appended to DefaultValidator.Rules in defaults.go — never create ad-hoc Validator instances in app code.
patterns.example: type AllowedAccountSetsRule struct { Sets [][]ledger.AccountType }; func (r AllowedAccountSetsRule) Validate(tx TxView) error { ... }

patterns.name: TxView as the rule's only input
patterns.description: NewTxView converts []ledger.EntryInput into []EntryView (with pre-decoded Route), exposing AccountTypes(), EntriesOf(accountType), HasAccountTypes(...). Rules must use TxView — never the raw EntryInput slice.
patterns.example: view, _ := NewTxView(entries); for _, rule := range v.Rules { rule.Validate(view) }

patterns.name: HasAccountTypes guard before rule logic
patterns.description: Every rule targeting specific account types checks tx.HasAccountTypes(r.From, r.To) first and returns nil when those types are absent. Omitting the guard causes false positives on unrelated transactions.
patterns.example: func (r RequireFlowDirectionRule) Validate(tx TxView) error { if !tx.HasAccountTypes(r.From, r.To) { return nil }; ... }

patterns.name: RequireSameRouteRule for cross-account field consistency
patterns.description: Ensures entries on two account types share the same RouteField values (currency, tax_code, features, cost_basis). Declared as Left/Right/Fields triplets in DefaultValidator; uses requireMatchingRouteFields helper.
patterns.example: RequireSameRouteRule{Left: ledger.AccountTypeCustomerFBO, Right: ledger.AccountTypeCustomerReceivable, Fields: []RouteField{RouteFieldCurrency, RouteFieldTaxCode}}

patterns.name: FuncRule for test-only rules
patterns.description: FuncRule wraps a plain function as a RoutingRule for test scenarios without a named struct. Never use in production code.

patterns.name: ErrRoutingRuleViolated with WithAttrs for all errors
patterns.description: All rule violations return ledger.ErrRoutingRuleViolated.WithAttrs(models.Attributes{...}) with a 'reason' key plus context. Never return plain fmt.Errorf from a RoutingRule.

key_file_guides.role: Core types: RoutingRule interface, Validator (implements ledger.RoutingValidator), FuncRule, and all concrete rule structs (AllowedAccountSetsRule, RequireFlowDirectionRule, RequireSameRouteRule, RequireAccountAuthorizationStatusRule, RequireReceivableAuthorizationStageRule, RequireAccruedCostBasisTranslationRule, RequireFBOCostBasisTranslationRule, RequireUniqueSubAccountsRule).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All rules skip gracefully when relevant account types are absent (HasAccountTypes first) — intentional permissive-when-unrelated behaviour; do not remove these guards.

key_file_guides.role: EntryView (wraps EntryInput + pre-decoded Route), TxView (slice of EntryView with AccountTypes/EntriesOf/HasAccountTypes/HasAccountType projections), and optional field comparators (optionalStringEqual, optionalDecimalEqual).
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntryView.Route() returns the decoded route from entry.PostingAddress().Route().Route() — use it for route comparisons, not the raw SubAccountRoute.

key_file_guides.role: DefaultValidator — the canonical production validator with all 12+ rules pre-composed in order; the single Validator used by historical.Ledger.CommitGroup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a rule here affects every CommitGroup in production. Test bidirectionally with TestDefaultValidator_* cases.

key_file_guides.role: Acceptance/rejection scenario table for DefaultValidator; addressForRoute constructs PostingAddress via ledger.BuildRoutingKey + ledgeraccount.NewAddressFromData.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New tests must use addressForRoute and require.ErrorContains(t, err, 'ledger routing rule violated') for rejections.

anti_patterns: Accessing ledger.EntryInput.PostingAddress().Route().Route() directly in rule code instead of EntryView.Route().
Creating a new Validator with a subset of rules instead of extending DefaultValidator.Rules — silently removes production constraints.
Adding DB calls, external I/O, or mutable state inside RoutingRule.Validate — rules must be pure functions of TxView.
Skipping the HasAccountTypes guard in a rule — false positives when relevant accounts are absent.
Returning plain fmt.Errorf instead of ledger.ErrRoutingRuleViolated.WithAttrs — breaks the error-type contract used by callers.

decisions.decision: Rules are applied sequentially and fail-fast (first error) rather than accumulating all violations.
decisions.rationale: Routing violations are programmer errors or abuse, not user-facing validation needing full accumulation; fail-fast simplifies rule logic.

decisions.decision: TxView pre-decodes the Route from SubAccountRoute.Route() at construction time.
decisions.rationale: Avoids repeated decode calls inside each rule and simplifies field-comparison helpers.

decisions.decision: DefaultValidator is a package-level var, not a constructor function.
decisions.rationale: Rules are stateless structs; a single shared instance is safe and avoids per-CommitGroup allocation.

code_examples.scenario: Validate entries before booking a transaction (production usage)

openmeter/ledger/testutils.purpose: Reusable test fixtures for ledger integration tests: Deps wires concrete account/resolver/historical-ledger adapters directly (no app/common), and IntegrationEnv spins up a real Postgres DB, runs migrations, creates a test customer, provisions all account types, and exposes typed sub-account helpers.

patterns.name: Build deps from constructors, not Wire
patterns.description: InitDeps wires accountadapter.NewRepo, accountservice.New, historicaladapter.NewRepo, resolversadapter.NewRepo, and resolvers.NewAccountResolver directly — never via app/common — to avoid import cycles.
patterns.example: repo := accountadapter.NewRepo(db); accountService := accountservice.New(repo, locker); historicalLedger := historical.NewLedger(historicalRepo, accountService, accountService, routingrules.DefaultValidator)

patterns.name: IntegrationEnv as the single test harness entry point
patterns.description: All ledger integration tests call NewIntegrationEnv(t, namespacePrefix) to get a provisioned namespace, customer, and account set. Never replicate DB setup, migration, or account provisioning inline.

patterns.name: Use t.Context() throughout
patterns.description: Every Ent call and service method in IntegrationEnv and Deps passes t.Context(), not context.Background(), tying cancellation and DB connections to the test harness.

patterns.name: Freeze clock in NewIntegrationEnv
patterns.description: Time is frozen with clock.FreezeTime(now) and t.Cleanup(clock.UnFreeze) so temporal ledger queries (balance snapshots, period windows) are deterministic.

patterns.name: Typed sub-account helpers per account type
patterns.description: FBOSubAccount, ReceivableSubAccount, AccruedSubAccount, WashSubAccount, EarningsSubAccount, BrokerageSubAccount call GetSubAccountForRoute with the correct typed params. Never construct SubAccount directly.

patterns.name: SumBalance reads settled balance only
patterns.description: SumBalance calls HistoricalLedger.GetSubAccountBalance and returns .Settled(). Tests needing pending/unsettled components must call GetSubAccountBalance directly.
patterns.example: sum := env.SumBalance(t, fboSubAccount) // settled balance as alpacadecimal.Decimal

key_file_guides.role: Wires the three concrete ledger services (AccountService, ResolversService, HistoricalLedger) from a raw *entdb.Client and *slog.Logger; exported Deps struct exposes all three for direct test use.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The single accountservice.New call before historicalLedger construction is load-bearing — historical.NewLedger receives accountService as both the internal service and Querier. Do not collapse or reorder these calls.

key_file_guides.role: Full integration harness: DB via omtestutils.InitPostgresDB, migrations via migrate.New+Up, customer row inserted via raw Ent, account provisioning via ResolversService.CreateCustomerAccounts and EnsureBusinessAccounts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Customer row is inserted via raw Ent (not customer.Service) so the package stays independent of app/common and customer/service. Do not switch to customer.Service here.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set — causes import cycles and couples helpers to the full DI graph.
Constructing ledger.SubAccount literals directly instead of the typed IntegrationEnv helper methods.
Using context.Background() instead of t.Context() — leaks connections and severs cancellation.
Skipping clock.FreezeTime in tests with temporal ledger queries — non-deterministic balance windows.
Calling migrate.Up more than once per IntegrationEnv instance — reuse the single env per test.

decisions.decision: Single accountservice.New call before historicalLedger construction (no two-phase pattern).
decisions.rationale: historical.NewLedger receives accountService as both its internal service and Querier; one instance passed twice is correct and avoids circular dependency.

decisions.decision: Direct Ent customer row insertion instead of customer.Service in NewIntegrationEnv.
decisions.rationale: Keeps testutils independent of app/common and customer/service, preventing import cycles per the test-003 enforcement rule.

decisions.decision: Typed sub-account helper methods per account type.
decisions.rationale: Wrapping GetSubAccountForRoute in named helpers ensures routing-rule params are always correct per account class and keeps tests readable.

code_examples.scenario: Set up and use a full ledger integration test
